success_surface: Row deletion based on conditional value filtering with robust type handling and structural preservation
effective_behaviors: Perform exploratory inspection of workbook structure before implementing modifications
Use case-insensitive sheet name matching to handle naming variations robustly
Implement type-safe value comparisons with explicit conversion and exception handling
Delete rows in reverse order to maintain correct row indexing during batch removals
Create file backup via copy-before-save workflow to preserve original data integrity
Print verbose row-by-row analysis for validation of deletion decisions before execution
evidence: Agent loaded workbook with openpyxl and printed sheet names and row previews before writing modification code
Agent attempted multiple sheet name variants ('Imported Data', 'imported Data') showing awareness of case sensitivity issues
After TypeError '<' not supported between str and int, agent added try/except blocks for safe numeric conversion
Final code deletes rows using sorted(rows_to_delete, reverse=True) to maintain index integrity
Used shutil.copy(input_file, output_path) before wb.save() to ensure backup exists
Printed --- Row Analysis --- section showing each row's value, numeric status, and DELETE/KEEP decision

memory_items.title: Pre-execution Workbook Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect the target workbook's sheets, rows, columns, and sample data before applying modifications
memory_items.generalization: Load and print structural metadata (sheet names, max dimensions, sample rows) to validate assumptions about headers, data types, and edge cases like None values or empty cells

memory_items.title: Robust Sheet Name Resolution
memory_items.lesson: Sheet names may vary in case or contain unexpected formatting; use flexible matching strategies
memory_items.generalization: Implement case-insensitive searches or fallback lookup patterns when accessing worksheets by name rather than hardcoding exact capitalization

memory_items.title: Type-Safe Numeric Comparisons
memory_items.lesson: Spreadsheet cells often contain mixed types (str, int, float, None); unsafe comparisons cause runtime errors
memory_items.generalization: Wrap all numeric condition checks in try/except blocks, explicitly convert string-like numbers, and skip non-numeric values rather than letting comparison operators fail

memory_items.title: Index-Aware Batch Deletions
memory_items.lesson: Deleting rows shifts subsequent row indices downward, causing off-by-one errors if processed forward
memory_items.generalization: Collect all rows to delete first, then iterate in descending order when calling deletion methods to preserve correct cell references

memory_items.title: File Backup Workflow
memory_items.lesson: Direct overwrite of input files risks permanent data loss if modifications fail or produce incorrect results
memory_items.generalization: Copy the original file to an output location before applying changes, saving the modified workbook to the destination path only after confirming success

memory_items.title: Verbose Decision Logging
memory_items.lesson: Debugging row-level transformations requires visibility into why specific rows were kept or removed
memory_items.generalization: Log each candidate row's identifier, extracted value, type check result, and final action before executing modifications to enable post-hoc verification

edits.content:
## Additional Best Practices from Successful Trajectories

### Exploration Before Modification
- Load and inspect workbook metadata (sheets, dimensions, sample content) before writing transformation logic.
- This validates schema assumptions and surfaces edge cases like missing headers or mixed data types.

### Flexible Sheet Access
- Use case-insensitive lookup patterns when referencing worksheets by name.
- Hardcoded capitalization leads to failures when source files vary in naming conventions.

### Defensive Type Handling
- All numeric comparisons must wrap potential conversions in try/except blocks.
- Check isinstance() before arithmetic or range operators; treat non-numeric values as keep-cases rather than crash-points.

### Safe Batch Row Removal
- Always process row deletions in reverse order (highest index to lowest).
- Deleting row N shifts all subsequent rows down; forward iteration produces duplicate deletions or skipped rows.

### Preserve Original Data
- Copy the input file to an output path before applying any writes.
- Only overwrite the original if the agent is required to do so; otherwise write exclusively to a new filename.

### Transparent Action Logging
- Print per-row evaluation summaries showing value, type classification, and keep/delete decision.
- Verbose output enables humans to audit transformation correctness without re-running experiments.
